one_law: Founder intent must become code, policy, proof, or approved output. It must not become copied phrases, hardcoded examples, or fake-green tests.

stages.name: input_capture
stages.purpose: Save founder text, timestamp, source surface, context hash, and candidate output if present.

stages.name: intent_gate
stages.purpose: Classify founder text before any implementation.

stages.name: meaning_compiler
stages.purpose: Translate founder language into behavior, invariant, UI copy, eval, machine change, or refusal.

stages.name: architecture_mapper
stages.purpose: Route the meaning to Brain, retrieval, live tools, guardrails, widget, Chat Unify, Hub/Form, Proof Pack, or docs.

stages.name: draft_builder
stages.purpose: Produce a candidate output or implementation spec.

stages.name: critic_loop
stages.purpose: Reject literal copying, hardcoded examples, fake green, public voice drift, internal leakage, weak proof, and missing benefit.

stages.name: machine_checks
stages.purpose: Run bounded checks appropriate to the scope: schema, eval, guardrail, lint, or dry-run classifier.

stages.name: approval_gate
stages.purpose: Return APPROVED, APPROVED_SPEC, RETURN_TO_AGENT, FIX_DISK, FIX_MACHINES, or ASK_FOUNDER.

stages.name: receipt
stages.purpose: Persist verifiable receipt with input hash, interpretation, route, checks, verdict, and next action.

intent_classes.description: Founder describes a desired system property or architecture state.
Founder explicitly asks for wording shown to visitors.
intent_classes.example: Write homepage copy that says SourceA runs real builds.

intent_classes.description: Founder gives examples that expose a general capability.
intent_classes.example: Farsi and Spanish were examples; Brain should translate generally.

intent_classes.description: Founder states a rule, prohibition, or mandatory boundary.
intent_classes.example: Brain must never leak internal repo paths.

intent_classes.description: Founder expresses frustration that points to a system bug.
intent_classes.example: You are explaining very weird and confusing.

intent_classes.description: Founder asks to build, fix, deploy, or run a bounded task.
Founder approves a plan, output, or action.
Intent cannot be safely resolved without a clarifying question.

failure_classes.description: Founder architecture language was copied into public code/copy instead of translated into behavior.
Implementation handles only the named examples instead of the general capability.
Tests pass only because the examples were hardcoded.
No eval, receipt, validator, or schema proves the output.
Output sounds inhuman, self-promotional, detached, or internal.
Output exposes internal paths, secrets, config, ports, incidents, or implementation details.
Output does not explain why it matters to the founder/user.

verdicts.APPROVED_SPEC: Interpretation/spec is approved; implementation can proceed.
verdicts.RETURN_TO_AGENT: Agent draft has quality/intent issues; rewrite using return_package.
verdicts.FIX_DISK: Disk, source, eval, or rule layer must be fixed first.
verdicts.FIX_MACHINES: Machine, validator, or integration drift must be fixed first.

canonical_cases.message: Farsi and Spanish are examples. Brain should translate generally.
Write visitor copy for the Forge Terminal CTA.
Brain must never show internal docs or config files to strangers.
This sounds weird and confusing for a stranger.
Implement the plan and validate it.

proof_requirements: route includes at least one concrete layer
approved output distinguishes copy vs code vs eval
